sharedEntities.description: Core entity representing loan data with attributes like loan_id, mpl_id, and loan_type; e2e-tests use it for simulation while data-utils maps it to accounts
Maps loans to financial accounts; defined in data-utils and verified in e2e tests
Configuration of accounts used for financial operations; data-utils resolves them while e2e-tests validates their behavior
Main database provisioned by IaC and used by e2e tests for validation scenarios

dataFlows.name: Account Resolution Flow
dataFlows.description: Process of mapping loans to appropriate accounts for financial operations
steps: data-utils calls COS Lending Accounting API to retrieve account configurations
data-utils maps loan details to specific accounts
account mappings are stored in SellingDB (provisioned by IaC)
e2e-tests validate the account mappings through simulated transactions

dataFlows.name: End-to-End Loan Processing
dataFlows.description: Complete loan lifecycle testing from origination through purchase
steps: e2e-tests initialize test environment using infrastructure provisioned by IaC
test simulates loan origination and processing using cos-simulator
account resolution occurs through data-utils integration
financial transactions execute against provisioned infrastructure
results are validated against expected outcomes

integrationPoints.description: E2E tests execute against AWS resources (Aurora DB, S3, SQS) provisioned by the IaC repository
Data utils service reads/writes to Aurora PostgreSQL database provisioned by IaC repository
E2E tests validate account resolution logic provided by data-utils through account-resolver API integration

patterns.description: AWS resources defined and provisioned using Terragrunt for consistent environments
Complete simulation of loan processing to validate business outcomes
Service pattern to map loans to their appropriate financial accounts
Used for reliable transfer processing ensuring data consistency

ambiguities: How SellingDB in cos-lending-selling-data-utils maps to specific Aurora database instances defined in iac-cos-lending-selling
Which specific S3 buckets from iac-cos-lending-selling are used by the e2e tests for test data storage
How the cos-simulator referenced in e2e-tests is deployed - whether it's part of the IaC definition
Whether the LoanActionsAccounts API in data-utils and e2e-tests are the same implementation or separate implementations
